title: Oh, Sweet Content!
content:
Oh, sweet content, that turns the labourer's sweat
     To tears of joy, and shines the roughest face;
    How often have I sought you high and low,
     And found you still in some lone quiet place;

    Here, in my room, when full of happy dreams,
     With no life heard beyond that merry sound
    Of moths that on my lighted ceiling kiss
     Their shadows as they dance and dance around;

    Or in a garden, on a summer's night,
     When I have seen the dark and solemn air
    Blink with the blind bats' wings, and heaven's bright face
     Twitch with the stars that shine in thousands there.
meaning: About:   William Henry Davies or W. H. Davies  was a Welsh poet and writer. Davies spent a significant part of his life as a tramp or vagabond in the United States and United Kingdom, but became known as one of the most popular poets of his time. The principal themes in his work are the marvels of nature, observations about life's hardships, his own tramping adventures and the various characters he met. Davies is usually considered as one of the Georgian poets, although much of his work is atypical of the style and themes adopted by others of the genre.
